mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates compress equity discount rates and lower corporate borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.5% indicates persistent labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns exceed +1.5% in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal synchronized consumer and construction demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive delta condition yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects immediate capacity utilization improvements and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rising INDPRO months, or the signal decays to zero predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 230.0 signals accelerating inflationary pressures that force markets to price in imminent monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns remain positive in >65% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in a piecewise regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a walk-forward validation framework.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion that immediately boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of positive GDP growth quarters, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index below 98.0 indicates severe manufacturing contraction and supply-side weakness that depresses corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of observations when INDPRO falls below 98.0, or the threshold condition fails to separate return distributions at a 95% confidence level.
